source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage / Crane Incident

event.incident_type: Structural Damage / Crane Lifting Device Incident
actions_taken: Crane operator swung crane back over incident site to lift tubing
Onboard medic attended to injured person
Injured person transported to rig hospital
Medevac helicopter evacuation approximately 2 hours after incident
Emergency surgery performed with pins inserted in three fingers
Partial amputation of middle finger performed
event.summary: On 29-OCT-2015 at 1740 hours, the Deck Coordinator sustained severe crush injuries to his left hand while attempting to relocate two joints of damaged production tubing on the Diamond Ocean Blackhornet. The tubing was being set on an uneven bundle of 6 5/8 inch piping in the pipe bay area. Despite warnings from two Roustabouts that the surface was not level and the tubing would roll, the Deck Coordinator continued the operation. When the tubing began to roll, the Deck Coordinator positioned himself between the tubing and a cargo box and attempted to wedge angle iron under the tubing. The weight of the tubing caused the angle iron to flip, crushing his left hand. The tubing continued to roll, pinning him between the cargo box and tubing until the crane lifted the tubing to free him.

threats.name: Worker positioned in pinch point area

consequences.name: Crush injury to hand and fingers

controls.name: Flagger communication with Crane Operator
controls.barrier_role: Maintain safe communication during lifting operations

supporting_text: The Deck Coordinator was tasked with being the 'Flagger' for the operation. Through the use of hand signals and a handheld radio, his job was to maintain communication with the Crane Operator throughout the lift.
The Deck Coordinator, for reasons unknown, failed to acknowledge the Roustabouts warning and continued flagging the Crane Operator to come down with the load.

controls.name: Pre-job hazard assessment and site inspection
controls.barrier_role: Identify unsuitable placement surface before load is set

supporting_text: Once they arrived to the pipe bay area, the Roustabouts realized that the bundle of pipe they were planning to set the tubing on was not level and would not likely allow the tubing to be set down without trying to roll off.
The Roustabouts approached the Deck Coordinator with their concerns and suggested that they find an alternative location to set the tubing.

controls.name: Stop Work Authority
controls.barrier_role: Empower workers to halt unsafe operations

supporting_text: STOP work authority not practiced as intended.
Both Roustabouts urged the Deck Coordinator to move out of the area as it was dangerous due to the unsecured tubing.
The IP was told a second time by the Roustabouts that his position was unsafe but their concerns were once again ignored.

controls.name: Load securing and stabilization procedures
controls.barrier_role: Prevent load movement during placement and work

supporting_text: Attempting to work with an unsecured load.
As the pipe was set down and the chokes of the slings began to loosen, the tubing began to roll off of the piping as the Roustabouts had expected.
The rolling of the tubing was stopped when one of the Roustabouts wedged a piece of wood under the tubing.

controls.name: Supervision and direct oversight of lifting operations
controls.barrier_role: Ensure safe execution of lifting tasks and respond to hazards

supporting_text: The Crane Operator, who was the person over the job, was unable to be directly involved due to having to operate the crane.
The Toolpusher contacted the Crane Operator and informed him that two joints of damaged production tubing needed to be relocated.

controls.name: Worker positioning and exclusion zones
controls.barrier_role: Keep workers out of pinch points and crush hazard areas

supporting_text: Poor Body Placement: The injured employee positioned himself between the tubing and the cargo box.
In an attempt to assess the situation, the Deck Coordinator positioned himself between the tubing and the cargo box.
Upon his return, the IP again positioned himself in between the cargo box and the tubing.

controls.name: Emergency response and rescue procedures
controls.barrier_role: Rapidly extract injured worker and provide medical care

supporting_text: Some members of the drill crew noticed the incident and quickly flagged to the Crane Operator to swing back over the site.
With the use of the crane, the crew was able to lift one end of the tubing up enough to allow the IP to free himself.
The IP was met at the scene of the incident by the onboard Medic and brought to the rig hospital to prepare for an evacuation.
The Medivac helicopter arrived on location approximately 2 hours following the incident.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
